fahrzeug.title: EV Assistant - Vehicle
fahrzeug.description: Step 1/7: vehicle basics. Manufacturer, model, and usable battery capacity are required — manufacturer and model together form the device name in the HA device list. Usable battery capacity: the value actually available for driving/charging — NOT the often larger gross/factory figure many manufacturers advertise (e.g. many models: 58 kWh gross, but only 52 kWh usable). This value directly determines how many kWh one percentage point of SoC increase represents — enter it wrong, and every energy estimate is wrong. Charge efficiency is only a starting value (optional) — it's replaced automatically once enough real home charging sessions are available (see step 3). First registration date is purely informational. The odometer is also the basis for automatic trip detection (Fahrtenbuch, step 6) and the distance basis for the optional cost comparison in step 7.

data.odo_entity: Odometer entity (trip detection in step 6 + distance basis for cost comparison in step 7)
data.usable_kwh: Usable battery capacity (kWh, net — NOT the gross factory figure)
data.charge_efficiency: Charge efficiency (AC->battery, starting value only until automatic calibration)

grundsignale.title: EV Assistant - Core Signals
grundsignale.description: Step 2/7: the two mandatory signals. * At least one source is required per signal: HA entity (takes priority) OR MQTT topic. Stellantis example: SoC entity = sensor.mein_auto_battery. Home-charging entity as a power sensor (e.g. wallbox charging power from evcc/Warp): from 0.1 kW counts as "charging". Important: this ONLY provides the yes/no signal "currently charging at home" — it is NOT the same charging power as in step 3 (that one deliberately comes from the vehicle rather than the wallbox, so it also works away from home during an external charge). Convert sensors in a different unit (e.g. watts) to kW via the template field, e.g. {{ value | float / 1000 }}.

ladeleistung.title: EV Assistant - Charging Power
ladeleistung.description: Step 3/7: optional charging-power and wallbox-energy measurement — two different sources for two different purposes. Charging power is your vehicle's current instantaneous charging power in watts or kW (not a meter, typically vehicle telemetry rather than a wallbox sensor — which means it also reports values during an external charge away from home, where your own wallbox naturally can't measure anything) — it improves the energy estimate of an external charge over a pure SoC-delta estimate. If your sensor reports AC-side (charge-point) power, leave "charging power is AC-side" enabled (default); if it reports DC/battery-side power, disable it. The wallbox energy meter, by contrast, is the opposite: a cumulative kWh meter of your own wallbox (like a regular electricity meter, not an instantaneous value) — it's only relevant for home charging and enables automatic calibration of the charge efficiency from real home charging sessions, as well as the home-charging cost in step 7.

data.power_is_ac: Charging power is AC-side (charge point) rather than DC-side (battery)
data.wallbox_energy_entity: Wallbox energy meter (cumulative kWh meter of your wallbox, optional)

ausgabe.title: EV Assistant - Output
ausgabe.description: Step 4/7: how you're additionally notified about a detected external charge. A notification in Home Assistant's "Notifications" area ALWAYS appears automatically, regardless of these two optional fields. The notify service additionally sends a push notification (e.g. to your phone); the MQTT topic additionally publishes the raw data as JSON for your own automations/integrations. Both are entirely optional.

data.publish_topic: MQTT topic for events (optional, for your own automations)
data.notify_service: notify service for push notification (optional, in addition to the automatic notification)

erkennung.title: EV Assistant - Detection
erkennung.description: Step 5/7: fine-tuning of external-charge detection. The default values work for most cases — only change them if you know what you're doing. Sequence: a charge starts once the SoC rises above the last resting value by the start threshold; it ends either when the SoC drops below the highest value reached by more than the "SoC drop", or when no new high has been reached for the "timeout without a new SoC high" duration (e.g. because the battery is full). "SoC noise" tolerates normal sensor jitter while tracking the high point and must therefore ALWAYS be smaller than the start threshold — otherwise ordinary noise alone can trigger a false detection.

data.start_delta: Start threshold (%, how much SoC increase counts as a charge starting)
data.noise: SoC noise (%, tolerance against sensor jitter — must be smaller than the start threshold!)
data.idle_timeout_s: Timeout without a new SoC high (s, ends the charge even without a SoC drop)
data.drop_ends: SoC drop ends charge immediately (%, relative to the highest value reached so far)

fahrtenbuch.title: EV Assistant - Trip Log
fahrtenbuch.description: Step 6/7: fine-tuning of automatic trip detection (Fahrtenbuch), which uses the same odometer sensor from step 1. The default values work for most cases. Sequence: a trip begins as soon as the odometer increases again after a period of standing still, and ends once the odometer hasn't increased for the given standing-still duration (the car is parked). Detected trips shorter than the minimum distance are discarded (filters out sensor rounding noise). Detected trips are then confirmed manually with a start/end location (service ev_assistant.log_trip). Optional: a person or device_tracker entity (e.g. your phone) provides a suggestion for the start/end location based on its HA zone (e.g. "Home") — this doesn't replace the manual entry, it only prefills it.

data.trip_min_km: Minimum distance (km, shorter detected trips are discarded)
data.trip_idle_timeout_s: Standing-still duration until trip end (s, the trip is considered finished without a new odometer reading)
data.gps_entity: Location suggestion (optional, person or device_tracker entity, e.g. your phone)

vergleich.title: EV Assistant - Cost Comparison
vergleich.description: Step 7/7: optional cost comparison against a combustion-engine car. All fields optional — without them the savings sensors stay unknown. Home-charging costs additionally require a configured wallbox energy measurement (step 3). Home electricity price and fuel price: each either a fixed value OR an entity (e.g. a dynamic tariff sensor or a fuel-price sensor) — the entity takes priority if both are set.

data.home_price_entity: Home electricity price entity (overrides the fixed price if set)
data.verbrenner_price_entity: Fuel price entity (overrides the fixed price if set)

error.no_soc_source: Please provide a SoC source: either an entity OR an MQTT topic.
error.no_home_source: Please provide a home-charging source: either an entity OR an MQTT topic. Without this signal, every SoC increase is falsely detected as an external charge, including normal charging at home.
